title: 6 Client Centre User Templates
body_text:
6	Client Centre User Templates
6.1	Overview of Client Centre User Templates
Client Centre Users can be created individually or from a template. CPS Admin Security Officers choose the set-up method when creating a new Client Centre User. See Section 5.2, Creating a Client Centre User, for details.
Templates are useful when Client Centre Users with similar permissions are created on a regular basis (e.g., new staff) or when a new group of Client Centre Users with similar permissions is created.
Templates contain common set-up details such as the realm, the address, the transit number, and a list of accessible applications. Personal details are entered when the template is used to create a Client Centre User.
Templates can be copied, modified, or deleted, and template ownership can be transferred to another CPS Admin Security Officer.
6.2	Client Centre User Template Rules
Client Centre User templates are subject to the following rules:
A CPS Admin Security Officer can use a template to create a Client Centre User if they have administrative rights to all the applications associated with the template. Templates marked with an asterisk arenât available to the CPS Admin Security Officer because they include applications to which the CPS Admin Security Officer doesnât have administrative rights.
The CPS Admin Security Officer who creates a template is the template owner.
The template owner is the only CPS Admin Security Officer who can modify the template or transfer ownership of the template.
A CPS Admin Security Officer can view and copy any template. If a CPS Admin Security Officer copies a template that contains applications to which they donât have administrative access, the new template wonât include those applications.
Only the template owner and CPS Admin Security Officers who donât have a realm can delete a template.
Authorization isnât required to create, delete, modify, copy, or transfer ownership of a template.
Template names must be unique (within your financial institution).
A CPS Admin Security Officer can create a new template upon request or when a Client Centre User is created or modified.
Client Centre Users created from a template must be authorized by another CPS Admin Security Officer.
6.3	Creating a Client Centre User Template
To create a template:
Log in to CPS Admin as described in Section 7.4, Logging in to CPS Admin.
Select the
Add User Template
tab.
See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Add User Template Tab
Complete the Add User Template page as follows. See Figure 2.
â
Template
â Enter a unique template name.
â¡
Division/Branch Name
â Enter the branch name (optional).
â¢
Realm
â Decide whether to enter a realm on the template. If the CPS Admin Security Officers who will manage Client Centre Users created from the template donât have a realm, leave the Realm field blank. If the CPS Admin Security Officers who will manage Client Centre Users created from the template are assigned to a realm, enter the same realm on the template.
Note:	CPS Admin Security Officers who donât have a realm can manage all of your financial institutionâs Client Centre Users whether or not the Client Centre User has a realm. However, if the CPS Admin Security Officer has a realm, they can only manage Client Centre Users with the same realm.
â£
Status
â The default status type is âActiveâ.
â¤
E-mail
â Enter a generic email address if appropriate. Alternatively, leave the field blank and enter a private email address when a Client Centre User is created from the template.
Note:	The branch address is optional.
â¥
User Type
â Select
Credit Union Staff
or
Corporate Client
as the user type (optional).
â¦
User Group
â The User Group field may be used for applications that donât have a user management component. Details on how to complete this field are provided with the applicationâs procedures (optional). This field isnât typically used.
â§
Transit Number
â Enter the transit number if the Client Centre Users who will be set up from this template belong to the same branch (optional).
â¨
Applications Menu
â Select the check box next to each application that will be included in the template.
Note: 	The Applications Menu only lists the applications to which you have administrative rights.
Note:	Some applications are associated with editable parameters, (e.g., Operator ID, Transit Number). Complete the generic fields, if applicable, and refer to the applicationâs procedures for content requirements.
â©
Select
Submit
.
Figure 2: Create Template
6.4	Viewing, Copying, Modifying, and Deleting Templates
CPS Admin Security Officers can view and copy all templates regardless of realm settings and template ownership. The template owner is the only CPS Admin Security Officer who can modify a template. Only the template owner and CPS Admin Security Officers who donât have a realm can delete a template.
To view, copy, modify, or delete a template:
Log in to CPS Admin as described in Section 7.4, Logging in to CPS Admin.
Select the
Update User Template
tab. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Update User Template Tab
Select the applicable link on the Update User Template page as follows. See Figure 4.
Viewing a Template
â To view a template:
â
Select
View
beside a template.
Copying a Template
â The copy function allows CPS Admin Security Officers to create a new template based on an existing template. If a template is copied and it contains applications to which you donât have administrative access, the new template wonât include those applications. To copy a template:
â
Select
Copy
beside a template. Enter a new template name, update the fields if necessary, and then select
Submit
.
Modifying a Template
â To modify a template:
â
Select
Modify
beside a template.
â
Update the template as required and select
Submit
. See Section 6.3, Creating a Client Centre User Template, for a description of each editable field.
Deleting a Template
â To delete a template:
â
Select
Delete
beside a template and then select
OK
when prompted.
Figure 4: Managing Templates
6.5	Transferring Ownership of a Client Centre User Template
The template owner is the only CPS Admin Security Officer who can modify the template or transfer ownership of the template. Before a CPS Admin Security Officer changes roles or leaves your financial institution, they should transfer ownership of their templates to another CPS Admin Security Officer.
Tip
:	If the ownership of a template isnât transferred before the template owner leaves your financial institution, the template canât be modified or transferred. However, it can be copied and saved under a new name. All CPS Admin Security Officers can copy templates.
To transfer the ownership of a template:
Log in to CPS Admin as described in Section 7.4, Logging in to CPS Admin.
Select the
Transfer User Template Ownership
tab. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Transfer User Template Ownership Tab
Complete the Transfer User Template Ownership page as described below. See Figure 6.
â
User Template
â Select the template from the drop-down menu.
â¡
New Ownerâs Email Address
â Enter the email address of the new template owner.
Note:	If more than one user ID is associated with the email address, you will be prompted to select the appropriate user ID.
Figure 6: Transfer Ownership
Select
Submit
.
